step1_instruction1: Click "Generate Key Pair" below to create an EC key pair (prime256v1)
step1_instruction2: Copy the Public Key and go to fleetkey.net - create an account and get your subdomain (e.g. abc123.fleetkey.net)
step1_instruction3: Upload the Public Key to your FleetKey.net account. Tesla will download the key from there during registration.
step2_instruction1: Create a Fleet API Application at
step2_instruction2: Origin: https://<your-subdomain>.fleetkey.net (your full FleetKey subdomain, e.g. https://abc123.fleetkey.net)
step2_instruction4: Copy Client ID and Client Secret from the created app and enter them below together with your FleetKey domain
step3_instruction1: Click "Generate Auth Link" - a new browser tab opens with the Tesla login page
step3_instruction2: Log in with your Tesla account and authorize the app
step3_instruction3: After login you will see "Page Not Found" - this is expected! Copy the complete URL from the browser address bar
step3_instruction4: Paste the URL into the field below and click "Save and close"
step3_warning: Never share this URL with anyone! It grants access to your Tesla account.
step4_description: The Virtual Key is required to send commands to your vehicle (lock/unlock, climate, charging, etc.). Without it, you can only read vehicle data. You can do this step after the adapter is running.
step4_instruction1: Open the URL below on your phone in the Tesla App, or scan the QR code with your phone camera
step4_instruction2: The Tesla App will ask you to confirm adding a "third-party key"
step4_instruction3: Go to your vehicle and hold your key card to the center console to confirm the installation
step4_qr_hint: Scan this QR code with your phone to open the URL directly in the Tesla App
